items.title: Lawsuit against Costco's popular rotisserie chicken 'fatally flawed,' company's lawyers say
items.summary_auto: A proposed class-action lawsuit that says Costco "systematically cheated" customers by advertising its hugely popular Kirkland Signature seasoned rotisserie chicken as containing no preservatives is "fatally flawed" and should be thrown out, lawyers for the company say.

items.title: Toronto Maple Leafs hire Jim Hiller as head coach
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Maple Leafs have hired Jim Hiller as head coach, the club announced Wednesday.

items.title: Winnipeg-born Canadian released from U.S. immigration detention after 7 months in 'hellhole'
Iran-U.S. agreement leaks reveal plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz, 60-day window for nuclear talks
items.summary_auto: Iran will immediately take steps to reopen the Strait of Hormuz once a tentative deal with the U.S. to end the war is signed and will be allowed to sell its oil without restrictions, according to leaked copies of an interim agreement that officials say broadly matches the document.

items.title: How advanced drones, surveillance towers are helping to tackle remote Alberta crime
items.summary_auto: High-tech surveillance cameras, police drones and AI analytics are changing how law enforcement responds to property crime at remote, hard-to-access industrial sites across Alberta.

items.title: Police in Quebec municipalities issued thousands of fines for insulting officers
items.summary_auto: Quebec City police have largely driven the numbers, issuing 11,092 fines in six years. Now Montreal is mulling a bylaw of its own regulating interactions between police and citizens, but advocates say it could exacerbate tensions and police misconduct.

items.title: Inside Ukraine’s kill zone
items.summary_auto: The last stretch of road to the frontlines in the Russia-Ukraine war is often called the kill zone. It’s swarming with drones, littered with burnt-out vehicles, and populated by residents continuing to live their daily lives.

items.title: Lionel Messi ties men's World Cup goals record with a hat trick as Argentina tops Algeria
items.summary_auto: With a brilliant hat trick in a 3-0 win over Les Fennecs, Argentina's Lionel Messi moved into a tie with Germany's Miroslav Klose for the career scoring record at the men's World Cup.

items.title: Rare antique N.S. pharmacy signs auctioned off for $32,500
items.summary_auto: Six pieces of signs advertising for a pharmacy in southwest Nova Scotia were sold at an auction for over $30,000. Auctioneer said the techniques, design and the history they carry made them hot commdity.

items.title: World-first burn treatment helps Western University student recover from injuries suffered in fire
items.summary_auto: A Hamilton hospital says it has become the first in the world to use an experimental exosome treatment on a burn patient, helping a Western University student recover from severe injuries suffered in a London fraternity house fire.

items.title: Rebuilding efforts continue after N.L. wildfire destroyed nearly 200 homes last year
items.summary_auto: Almost a year after a devastating wildfire tore through the north shore of Conception Bay, destroying nearly 200 homes, people are rebuilding and taking stock of what was lost and isn't so easily replaced.

items.title: Canadians grapple with loss of Hockey Night in Canada's 'grand tradition'
Companies question cost of AI as tokenmaxxing spending adds up
items.summary_auto: Prominent tech businesses pushing to use AI intensively have been stung by sky high AI costs, especially for agentic chain-of-thought purposes. Now, they're looking at getting beyond experimentation to tokenomics: really breaking down the return on investment for big AI spending.

items.title: 'Major shock': Canadians grapple with loss of CBC's Hockey Night in Canada tradition
items.summary_auto: Canadians are grappling with the loss of a cultural touchstone after CBC announced Tuesday its Hockey Night in Canada broadcasts have come to an end.

items.title: Is it time Quebec's police watchdog make its reports public — like the rest of Canada?
items.summary_auto: Quebec’s police watchdog is the only police oversight body in Canada whose final reports are not made public. Some advocates and families who have lost loved ones following police interventions say that needs to change.

items.title: Students from 2 Canadian schools published Holocaust denial quotes in their yearbooks. Why it went unnoticed
items.summary_auto: Graduating students at two Canadian high schools submitted Holocaust denial quotes in their yearbooks this spring — and the quotes were even published before the schools took action.

items.title: Matchmaking and cash: South Korea's plan to avoid population decline
items.summary_auto: Canada also faces a record-low fertility rate, and experts are looking to South Korea to see what they're doing right after years of plummeting birthrates.

items.title: Carney says he's seen U.S.-Iran framework to end war, calls it a 'game changer' in CNN interview
items.summary_auto: Prime Minister Mark Carney says he's seen a copy of the preliminary deal struck between the U.S. and Iran to end hostilities in the Middle East and is calling it a "game changer."

items.title: How a satirical Bosnian song about the American dream became a World Cup anthem
items.summary_auto: When Bosnian band Dubioza kolektiv released U.S.A. in 2011, they never imagined their tongue-in-cheek song about disillusionment with the American dream would be repurposed into a certified World Cup hit.

items.title: Alberta judge dismisses bid to stall closure of supervised drug sites
items.summary_auto: A judge has dismissed a last-ditch legal effort to temporarily halt the closures of supervised consumption sites in Calgary and Lethbridge, citing a similar effort to stall another shuttered site.

items.title: Parliamentary committee recommends against MAID expansion for mental illness
items.summary_auto: A special parliamentary committee will recommend that the federal government indefinitely pause the expansion of medical assistance in dying for people whose sole underlying condition is a mental illness, CBC News has confirmed.

items.title: Erling Haaland scores twice, adds assist in World Cup debut as Norway tops Iraq 4-1 in Group I
items.summary_auto: Erling Haaland scored his first two World Cup goals, including one off a defensive blunder, and added an assist in his tournament debut Tuesday to propel Norway to a 4-1 victory over Iraq in Group I.

items.title: Extreme weather part of 400% hike in Alberta home insurance premiums over 20 years: StatsCan
items.summary_auto: New data from Statistics Canada suggests homeowners' insurance in Alberta increased nearly five times in a 20-year period — the highest increase of any province.

items.title: New Royal Canadian Navy boss says service must grow by up to 40%
items.summary_auto: The Royal Canadian Navy's sweeping modernization plans hinge on a challenge that can't be solved in a shipyard. Vice-Admiral Dan Charlebois says the service must grow by as much as 40 per cent to crew a new fleet of destroyers, submarines and support vessels now taking shape.

items.title: Town cancels parade, citing 'overwhelming' harassment after pro-Alberta float disallowed
items.summary_auto: A central Alberta town won't host its annual parade this year after organizers said a decision to disallow a pro-Alberta float led to a barrage of "online criticism, personal attacks, harassment, and abusive messages."

items.title: Trump spent $14M US to make reflecting pool look blue. Now the water is green
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump believed he had a solution to the reflecting pool's algae problems: painting its bottom a particular shade of blue. Less than two weeks after the repair work finished, the algae is back.

items.title: Canada's Max Crépeau doing his part as goalkeepers coming up big in World Cup
items.summary_auto: In the early days of this tournament, goalkeepers have found themselves the subject of hotter attention, the 18-yard box a proving ground more than a sanctuary.

items.title: Kylian Mbappe sparks France with two goals in 3-1 win over Senegal at the World Cup
items.summary_auto: Kylian Mbappe scored twice to move past Pele and Lionel Messi with 14 World Cup goals, Bradley Barcola added another and France rebounded from a surprisingly poor first half to beat Senegal 3-1 Tuesday in New Jersey.

items.title: Toronto man to plead guilty to conspiring to smuggle cocaine for drug network allegedly tied to Ryan Wedding
items.summary_auto: A Toronto man arrested for his role in conspiring to smuggle cocaine for a drug network allegedly tied to former Canadian Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding has taken a plea deal in the U.S.

items.title: Ghanaian midfielder Thomas Partey loses bid to enter Canada for World Cup
items.summary_auto: A Federal Court judge has dismissed Thomas Partey's bid for emergency relief after Canada denied the Ghanaian midfielder entry for the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Criminality not suspected after 12 kids sent to hospital in electrical incident at B.C. water park
Russian warship fired warning shots near U.K.-registered yacht in English Channel
items.summary_auto: A Russian warship fired warning shots near a U.K.-registered pleasure yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday, authorities said, an incident that caused no damage but illustrated heightened tensions between the two countries.

items.title: 11-year-old dies in suspected drowning during school field trip near Medicine Hat, Alta.
items.summary_auto: An 11-year-old child has died of a suspected drowning during a field trip to a regional park near Medicine Hat, Alta., on Monday. That led to a shutdown of pools across the city the following day.

items.title: Hundreds of families return home after crews bring West Kelowna, B.C., wildfire under control
The Grammys add 5 new categories, announce changes to best new artist
items.summary_auto: The academy on Tuesday announced the addition of best Asian pop music performance, best traditional pop vocal performance and best Latin categories, plus changes to the R&B and folk categories. The Grammys will also now allow artists to submit in the best new artist category four times, up from three.

items.title: Netflix announces Icebreaker, the latest hockey romance based on a bestselling novel
items.summary_auto: Netflix announced Monday that it’s adapting the bestselling book Icebreaker by English author Hannah Grace into a “college romance drama series." The young adult romance novel, published in 2022, is about an ambitious figure skater, Anastasia Allen, and a hunky hockey team captain, Nate Hawkins, who fall in love after they’re forced to share the ice.
